findings.issue: Generic dialogue beat; the sentence is only a stock hesitation label and could belong to any breathless messenger.
findings.suggestedFix: The runner dragged in another breath.

findings.issue: Generic dialogue beat; it stops at a stock facial label instead of giving a character-specific physical tell.
Hedge drag; the qualifier weakens a moment that should either commit to the expression or render it concretely.

findings.line: This had stopped being paperwork the moment she saw the hands.
findings.whatItRestates: Jaina's ink-stained, frost-split hands already tell Sylvanas this is field trouble rather than tidy diplomatic business.
findings.issue: Narrative over-translation; the sentence explains the conclusion after the concrete image already landed.

findings.line: Cyndia met her outside, braid dark at the temples, posture straight enough for respect and tired enough for truth.
findings.povMismatch: Sylvanas would clock discipline and fatigue, but "tired enough for truth" is a polished authorial gloss instead of her in-body read of another ranger.
findings.suggestedFix: Cyndia met her outside, braid dark at the temples, posture still straight despite the fatigue at her eyes.

findings.line: Everyone with ears had simply understood this had crossed from routine detention into whatever came next.
findings.povMismatch: The sentence generalizes the whole post's internal read from outside Sylvanas's body instead of staying with what she can register concretely in the yard.
findings.suggestedFix: Voices had dropped around the yard.

findings.line: Which made it either nonsense or the wrong kind of truth.
findings.missingPayload: Whether the runner's "rot" claim is empty nonsense or a ward warning described in the language of spoiled grain and wells.
findings.reason: Placeholder abstraction; the line reaches for weight through "truth" without cashing out what category of warning Sylvanas is actually considering.
findings.suggestedFix: Which made it either nonsense or a warning about the wards in the language of spoiled grain and wells.

findings.line: Frost-bitten knuckles and ink stains belonged to scholars and field workers, the sort who let the work chew at the body around it.
findings.failedQuestion: What does this cash out to?
findings.missingPayload: Long field hours that split hands, burn knuckles, and grind ink into the fingers.
findings.reason: Placeholder-noun stand-in; "the work" borrows intensity from a concrete image while refusing to name the actual bodily cost already implied by the scene.
findings.suggestedFix: Frost-bitten knuckles and ink stains belonged to scholars and field workers who let the job split their hands and stain their fingers.

findings.line: By the time the outpost came into full view, anger had worn down into a colder, more useful thing.
findings.missingPayload: Sylvanas's anger narrowing into hard focus on the outpost and the prisoner rather than a vague "thing."
findings.reason: Placeholder-noun stand-in; the sentence wants the authority of compression without naming the specific mental state it lands on.
findings.suggestedFix: By the time the outpost came into full view, anger had worn down into hard focus on the outpost.

findings.line: Cyndia met her outside, braid dark at the temples, posture straight enough for respect and tired enough for truth.
findings.missingPayload: The visible fatigue cue that tells Sylvanas Cyndia will answer plainly.
findings.reason: Unanchored interpretive summary; the sentence names "truth" instead of staying with the physical signs Sylvanas can actually read.
findings.suggestedFix: Cyndia met her outside, braid dark at the temples, posture still straight despite the fatigue at her eyes.

audits.cleanReason: No line in this chapter sets up a plain-speech or street-level translation exchange, so the guide had no candidates to audit.

summary.verdictReason: Pass B fails: more than three mild KILL findings remain across GPT-5 prose issues, narrator seep, and phantom concreteness, even though every hit has a low-difficulty fix.
